module-2-community-website-history.label: Module Project: Build Volunteer Project History
module-2-community-website-history.summary: Continue volunteer-project and build focused assignment and meeting-time history.

try_module_2_community_website_history_s.title: Assign the Training Desk
try_module_2_community_website_history_s.prompt: Open `volunteer-project/assignments.txt`. Find `Training desk: unassigned` and replace it with `Training desk: Riley`. Save the file and change nothing else. Then inspect only that unstaged file.
try_module_2_community_website_history_s.hint: Apply the exact assignment change and review it before staging.

help.concept: The file edit is fully specified; the project tests Git inspection, not content invention.
help.hint_1: Do not change the title or any other line.
help.hint_2: Inspect the saved working-tree change before staging.

try_module_2_community_website_history_s.starterCode:
Volunteer assignments

Welcome table: Sam
Training desk: unassigned
try_module_2_community_website_history_s.instructions: Open `volunteer-project/assignments.txt`. Find `Training desk: unassigned` and replace it with `Training desk: Riley`. Save the file and change nothing else. Then inspect only that unstaged file.

zoeskoul_setup_sh.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -eu
rm -rf -- volunteer-project
mkdir -p -- volunteer-project
git -C volunteer-project init -q -b main
git -C volunteer-project config --local user.name 'ZoeSkoul Learner'
git -C volunteer-project config --local user.email learner@zoeskoul.local
mkdir -p -- volunteer-project
printf %s '# Volunteer Project

Assignments and meeting times.
' > volunteer-project/README.md
mkdir -p -- volunteer-project
printf %s 'Volunteer assignments

Welcome table: Sam
Training desk: unassigned
' > volunteer-project/assignments.txt
mkdir -p -- volunteer-project
printf %s 'Volunteer meeting times

Orientation: Saturday 10:00 AM
' > volunteer-project/meeting-times.txt
mkdir -p -- volunteer-project
printf %s 'Old volunteer notes

Call list from last season.
' > volunteer-project/old-notes.txt
git -C volunteer-project add -A
GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-01T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-01T12:00:00Z git -C volunteer-project commit -q -m 'Start volunteer project'

git -C volunteer-project add -A
GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-01T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-01T12:00:00Z git -C volunteer-project commit -q -m 'Start volunteer project'

0.message: That action can discard or rewrite history. Use the safe, layer-specific workflow taught in this course.
Did you inspect the exact assignment change before staging it?

try_module_2_community_website_history_s.title: Commit the Assignment Change
try_module_2_community_website_history_s.prompt: Stage only `assignments.txt`, inspect its staged patch, and record it with the message `Update volunteer assignments`. Leave every other file outside this commit.
try_module_2_community_website_history_s.hint: Review the proposed assignment snapshot before recording it.

help.concept: The first Module 2 project purpose becomes its own commit.
help.hint_2: The working tree should be clean afterward.

try_module_2_community_website_history_s.starterCode:
Volunteer assignments

Welcome table: Sam
Training desk: Riley
try_module_2_community_website_history_s.solutionCode:
git add assignments.txt
git diff --staged -- assignments.txt
git commit -m "Update volunteer assignments"
try_module_2_community_website_history_s.instructions: Stage only `assignments.txt`, inspect its staged patch, and record it with the message `Update volunteer assignments`. Leave every other file outside this commit.

try_module_2_community_website_history_s.title: Stage and Review the Meeting-Time Change
try_module_2_community_website_history_s.prompt: Stage only `meeting-times.txt`, then inspect its staged patch. Do not commit yet.
try_module_2_community_website_history_s.hint: The staging area should contain only the meeting-time update.

help.concept: The staged diff is the final review of the next snapshot.
help.hint_2: Do not amend the earlier commit.

try_module_2_community_website_history_s.starterCode:

try_module_2_community_website_history_s.instructions: Stage only `meeting-times.txt`, then inspect its staged patch. Do not commit yet.

try_module_2_community_website_history_s.title: Audit the Volunteer Project History
try_module_2_community_website_history_s.prompt: Read the concise history, inspect the latest commit summary, and confirm that short status has no unfinished entries. The repository should now contain three focused commits.
try_module_2_community_website_history_s.hint: Verify the timeline, the newest snapshot, and the clean current state.

help.concept: This milestone proves the focused history before Module 3 continues the same repository.
help.hint_1: The newest commit should be Update volunteer meeting time.
help.hint_2: The final short status should be empty.

try_module_2_community_website_history_s.starterCode:
# Volunteer Project

Assignments and meeting times.
try_module_2_community_website_history_s.solutionCode:
git log --oneline
git show --stat --oneline HEAD
git status --short
try_module_2_community_website_history_s.instructions: Read the concise history, inspect the latest commit summary, and confirm that short status has no unfinished entries. The repository should now contain three focused commits.

moduleProject.description: Do not start over. The project opens from the canonical Module 1 snapshot. Apply two exact text edits, inspect each one, and record them as separate focused commits. The resulting three-commit history continues into Module 3.
moduleProject.title: Build Focused Volunteer Project History

intro-sketch.title: Continue the Volunteer Project
intro-sketch.bodyMarkdown: The project opens from its canonical Module 1 snapshot. Apply two exact text edits, inspect them, and record them as separate focused commits. The resulting three-commit history continues into Module 3.
